build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors. Returns 0 or 1; the rest of the score is multiplied by this gate.

requirements.description: The player skates with momentum-based physics: speed builds on downhill slopes and drains on uphill. The skater moves left/right with smooth rolling animation. Jumping launches the skater into the air with gravity. Landing on ramps preserves momentum. Score 0 if there is no momentum physics, if movement is constant speed regardless of terrain, or if there is no jump mechanic.

A trick system exists: button combinations trigger tricks (flip tricks in air, grind tricks near rails, grab tricks while airborne). Each trick has a name that pops up on screen and a distinct animation. At least three trick categories exist. Score 0 if there are no tricks, if tricks have no animation, or if fewer than 3 trick types exist.

A combo and score multiplier system exists: linking tricks without touching ground or bailing increases the multiplier. Landing cleanly banks the accumulated score; bailing loses the current combo. A combo meter shows current chain length and potential score. Score 0 if there is no combo system, if the multiplier does not increase with chains, or if bailing has no penalty.

At least three distinct parks exist with different layouts: a street course (rails, stairs, ledges), a vert ramp (half-pipes, bowls), and a combined mega park. Each park has unique visual theming and obstacle placement. Parks unlock progressively. Score 0 if there are fewer than 3 parks, if parks look identical, or if all parks are available from the start.

Career goals exist for each park: specific challenges like target scores, required trick sequences, or time limits. Completing goals unlocks new tricks and parks. A goals list shows current objectives and completion status. Score 0 if there are no goals, if goals have no rewards, or if there is no goals display.

Unlockable tricks expand the player's repertoire as goals are completed. New tricks are more complex and score higher. At least five total tricks are available across all categories. A trick list shows unlocked and locked tricks. Score 0 if there are fewer than 5 tricks total, if no tricks are unlockable, or if there is no trick list.

A park editor exists: the player can place ramps, rails, and obstacles on a grid to create custom parks. Placed elements snap to positions and are immediately skateable. At least three distinct placeable element types exist. Score 0 if there is no park editor, if placed elements are not functional, or if fewer than 3 element types exist.

The full skating loop is connected: Build speed, jump, perform trick, land cleanly, chain into next trick, bank combo score. Each step produces visible state changes. Score 0 if any step in the chain is missing or broken.

The full game loop is connected: Title screen, park selection, skate session, complete goals, unlock tricks/parks, park editor. The player can experience every node without restarting the application. Score 0 if any transition in the chain is missing or broken.

A title screen is present and styled: it shows the game name in a graffiti or street-art font, has at minimum a play button that is interactive and transitions to the game, and uses a non-default background (half-pipe silhouette, skate park imagery) — not plain Godot grey or a solid-colour fill. Score 0 if the game launches directly into gameplay with no title screen, or if the start button is non-functional.

Overall art-style coherence: every visible element — parks, skater, ramps, rails, UI panels — belongs to a single consistent urban/street visual language. The palette is bold and energetic; no clashing photorealistic assets or unrelated themes. Score 0 if the screen mixes incompatible styles, or if elements are obviously unthemed placeholder shapes.

Environment, character, and UI art quality: parks use textured or illustrated surfaces with visible ramp/rail detail; the skater is a recognisable sprite with distinct poses; UI elements (score, combo meter, goals) use styled, themed widgets — not raw unstyled Godot defaults. Score 0 if parks are uniform colour fills, if the skater is a solid-colour primitive, or if primary UI elements are obviously unstyled default widgets.

Animation continuity and polish: skating movement, trick rotations, grind sparks, landing impact, bail tumble, combo popup, and park transitions each play as a smooth multi-frame tween or sprite animation — no element snaps to its new state in a single frame. Scene transitions use a brief fade rather than an instant cut. Score 0 if any of these moments is instantaneous, or if transitions are hard cuts with no tween.
